user.title: Energy Sources
user.description: Step 1/2: Connect AURUM to your energy sensors. You need at least a grid meter sensor.

data_description.grid_power_entity: Your grid meter sensor (e.g. Shelly 3EM, SMA, Fronius, Kostal). Must report current power in watts. Positive = drawing from grid, negative = feeding into grid. This is the most important sensor — AURUM can't work without it.
data_description.pv_power_entity: How much power your solar panels are generating right now (in watts). Only used for display, not for calculations. Can be left empty.
data_description.battery_soc_entity: How full your home battery is right now (in percent, 0–100%). Leave empty if you don't have a battery — AURUM will send all surplus power directly to your devices.

battery.title: Battery Settings
battery.description: Step 2/2: How should AURUM handle your battery? If you don't have a battery, you can keep the defaults.

data_description.battery_capacity_wh: How large is your battery? Enter in watt-hours. Examples: 5,000 Wh = 5 kWh, 10,000 Wh = 10 kWh.
data_description.target_soc: At what charge level should all devices be allowed to run? Default: 80%. While the battery is below this, AURUM only runs devices with special permission.
data_description.min_soc: Below what charge level should ALL devices be turned off? Protects your battery from deep discharge. Default: 10%.
data_description.update_interval: How often AURUM checks sensors and controls devices (in seconds). 15 seconds works well for most setups. Usually doesn't need to be changed.

abort.already_configured: AURUM is already set up. You can configure it under Settings → Integrations.

data.action: What would you like to do?

settings.title: Energy & Battery
settings.description: Change your sensors and battery settings.

data_description.grid_power_entity: Your grid meter sensor in watts. Positive = drawing from grid, negative = feeding in.
data_description.pv_power_entity: Current solar production in watts (optional, display only).
data_description.battery_soc_entity: Current battery charge level in percent.
data_description.battery_capacity_wh: Battery size in watt-hours (e.g. 10,000 Wh = 10 kWh).
data_description.target_soc: Above this level, all devices are allowed. Below, individual device thresholds apply.
data_description.min_soc: Below this level, ALL devices are turned off (battery protection).
data_description.update_interval: How often AURUM checks and controls, in seconds. Default: 15s.

add_device.title: Add Device
add_device.description: Add a device that AURUM should control with solar power. Any smart plug that shows up in Home Assistant works.

data_description.name: Give the device a unique name, e.g. 'Washing Machine', 'Pool Pump' or 'EV Charger'. This name will appear as a sensor in Home Assistant.
data_description.switch_entity: The smart plug that turns this device on and off. Just pick it from the list — all available plugs and switches are shown.
data_description.power_entity: Does your smart plug measure power consumption? Then select its power sensor here. AURUM will use the real consumption instead of the estimate. Can be left empty.
data_description.nominal_power: How much power does this device use when running? Usually written on the device label. Examples: Washing machine ~ 2,000W, Pool pump ~ 500W, EV charger ~ 3,700W. Used as estimate when no power sensor is set.
data_description.priority: Which devices should get solar power first? Higher number = more important. Recommendation: 80–100 for washing machine/dishwasher, 50–70 for pool pump/water heater, 20–40 for EV charger.
data_description.soc_threshold: When the battery drops below the charge target: at what battery level is this device still allowed to run? Set to 0 if the device should only run with full surplus.
data_description.startup_detection: Enable for appliances that run programs (washing machines, dishwashers, dryers). AURUM automatically detects when a program starts and protects it from being interrupted — even when the sun goes behind clouds.
data_description.deadline: When does this device need to be finished? Example: 18:00 if laundry needs to be done by dinner time. AURUM will start the device in time — even without solar surplus. Leave empty if there's no time requirement.
data_description.estimated_runtime: How long does a typical cycle take? Examples: Washing machine ~ 120 min, Dishwasher ~ 150 min. Used together with the deadline so AURUM knows when to start.

remove_device.title: Remove Device
remove_device.description: Choose a device to remove from AURUM control.
